sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cao Rui was styled Yuanzhong.

He broke precedent by giving himself the temple name of Fierce Progenitor (烈祖), even though they were supposed to be given out after an emperor died. This is said to be the first case of an emperor giving himself a temple name.

full_text:
Cao Rui (Yuanzhong) 曹叡 (元仲)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Rui (曹叡), styledYuanzhong (元仲), was born in Pei. Emperor of Wei, he defended the dynasty from Shu and Wu, but was criticized for his spending. Cao Rui was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Cao Rui lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

Cao Rui Biography (ZZTJ Compilation)
BiographySource Undefined
Cao Rui was styled Yuanzhong.
He broke precedent by giving himself the temple name of Fierce Progenitor (烈祖), even though they were supposed to be given out after an emperor died. This is said to be the first case of an emperor giving himself a temple name.
